threats.title: Grok Build Uploads Entire Git Repositories to xAI Storage, Not Just Files It Reads
threats.summary: xAI's Grok Build coding CLI was uploading entire Git repositories, full commit history and all, to a Google Cloud Storage bucket run by xAI, not just the files a coding task needed. threats.title: U.S. Sanctions First VPN Service and Malware Cryptor Seller Over Ransomware Support
threats.summary: The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has designated two individuals and a VPN service provider for enabling ransomware actors' and other cybercriminals' malicious activities, including ransomware attacks against Americans. threats.title: 148 npm Packages Disguised as Student Proxies Turned Browsers Into a DDoS Botnet
threats.summary: A campaign of 148 npm packages disguised as student web proxies turned visitors' browsers into a distributed denial-of-service botnet for roughly two weeks in May, according to new research from JFrog. threats.title: Microsoft Maps Three Salesforce Attack Paths Tied to a Year of ShinyHunters Activity
threats.summary: Attackers whose methods line up with the data-extortion group ShinyHunters have spent the past year walking into corporate Salesforce environments without exploiting a single flaw in the platform. threats.title: CrashStealer macOS Malware Uses Notarized Dropper to Pass Gatekeeper Checks
threats.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called CrashStealer that's capable of harvesting sensitive data from compromised systems. Unlike other information stealers that are built on AppleScript droppers or Objective-C-based wrappers, CrashStealer is implemented in native C++, according to Jamf Threat Labs.
